name: RemindMe of This Tweet
tagline: Set reminders on any tweet. No account needed.
problem: Twitter/X killed off third-party reminder bots by jacking up API prices. No reliable way exists to bookmark a tweet and get reminded about it later. Existing extensions are sketchy, Web3-focused, or dead.
solution: A zero-infrastructure Chrome extension that injects a 'Remind me' button on every tweet. Reminders are stored in chrome.storage.sync (tied to Google account, survives reinstalls) and checked via a polling alarm. Notifications link back to the original tweet.

architecture.approach: Fully client-side. No server, no accounts, no API keys.
architecture.storage: chrome.storage.sync for cross-device persistence (100KB limit, ~1000+ reminders). Fallback to chrome.storage.local if sync quota exceeded.
architecture.polling: chrome.alarms fires every 30 minutes. Background service worker checks for due reminders and fires chrome.notifications.
architecture.injection: Content script uses MutationObserver on X's timeline to inject reminder buttons as tweets render.

core.name: Remind Me button injection
core.description: Content script injects a small clock/bell icon button into each tweet's action bar (next to like, retweet, etc). Uses MutationObserver to catch dynamically loaded tweets.

core.name: Time picker
core.description: Clicking the button opens a small popover with preset options: 1 hour, 4 hours, tomorrow morning, 1 week, 1 month, custom date/time. No natural language parsing in v1 — just a date/time picker for custom.

core.name: Reminder storage
core.description: Each reminder stored as { id, tweetUrl, tweetText (truncated, 280 chars), authorHandle, reminderTime, createdAt }. Stored in chrome.storage.sync, overflow to chrome.storage.local.

core.name: Polling alarm
core.description: A persistent chrome.alarms alarm fires every 30 minutes. Service worker checks all stored reminders against current time. Due reminders trigger a notification and get removed from storage.

core.name: Desktop notification
core.description: chrome.notifications.create with the tweet text preview and author handle. Clicking the notification opens the tweet URL in a new tab.

core.name: Popup dashboard
core.description: Extension popup shows a list of all pending reminders sorted by due date. Each entry shows tweet preview, author, due date, and a delete button. Empty state says 'No reminders set.'

nice_to_have.name: Visual indicator on reminded tweets
nice_to_have.description: If user scrolls past a tweet that already has a reminder set, the button shows as active/highlighted with the reminder date on hover.

nice_to_have.name: Snooze from notification
nice_to_have.description: Notification includes action buttons: 'Open Tweet' and 'Snooze 1 hour'.

nice_to_have.name: Badge count
nice_to_have.description: Extension icon shows badge with count of overdue/due-today reminders.

nice_to_have.name: Export/import reminders
nice_to_have.description: JSON export/import from the popup for backup purposes.

extension_structure.background.js: Service worker — alarm listener, notification handler, storage management
extension_structure.content.js: Content script — MutationObserver, button injection, popover UI
extension_structure.content.css: Styles for injected button and popover, matched to X's design language
extension_structure.popup.js: Dashboard logic — list, delete, edit reminders

technical_notes.dom_injection: X uses React with virtualized lists. Tweets are destroyed and recreated as user scrolls. MutationObserver on the timeline container must watch for childList changes and inject buttons on new tweet elements. Key selector target: article[data-testid='tweet'] or equivalent. These selectors WILL break — plan for resilience.
technical_notes.tweet_url_extraction: Each tweet article contains a time element with a link to the tweet. Extract href from a[href*='/status/'] within the tweet article.
technical_notes.author_extraction: Extract from the tweet URL or from user link elements within the tweet.
technical_notes.storage_limits: chrome.storage.sync: 100KB total, 8KB per item. At ~200 bytes per reminder, that's ~400 reminders per sync item, ~500 total across all items. More than enough. If somehow exceeded, fall back to chrome.storage.local (10MB default).
technical_notes.alarm_behavior: chrome.alarms minimum interval is 1 minute in MV3, but for battery/resource reasons use 30 minutes. On alarm fire, check all reminders — any with reminderTime <= Date.now() get fired and removed.
technical_notes.service_worker_lifecycle: MV3 service workers can be killed. Alarms will wake them. Do NOT rely on in-memory state — always read from storage.

design_guidelines.button: Small, unobtrusive. Match X's icon style (thin stroke, ~18-20px). Use a clock or bell icon. Muted gray, highlights on hover. Should feel native to X's UI.
design_guidelines.popover: Minimal. Dark theme to match X. Preset time buttons as pills/chips. Custom date/time picker below. Small footprint — shouldn't obscure the tweet.
design_guidelines.popup_dashboard: Clean list view. Dark theme. Each reminder shows: truncated tweet text, @handle, relative time until reminder ('in 3 days'), delete button. Sort by soonest first.
design_guidelines.notifications: Use tweet author's handle as title. Tweet text preview as body. Extension icon as notification icon.

risks: X changes their DOM structure (high probability) — button injection selectors will break. Mitigation: use multiple fallback selectors, fail gracefully.
chrome.storage.sync has write limits (MAX_WRITE_OPERATIONS_PER_MINUTE). Unlikely to hit for reminders but guard against it.
User clears all extension data — reminders lost. Mitigation: export feature (P2).
Long-duration reminders (months/years) rely on Chrome + Google account staying intact. No real mitigation other than the sync backup.
monetization: None. Free, open source. No accounts, no tracking, no ads. This is a utility that should've existed natively.
distribution: Chrome Web Store. Potentially Firefox Add-ons later (alarm and storage APIs are compatible via WebExtension standard).
